data.title: Golar LNG firms up 20-year FLNG charter in Argentina
data.description:
Golar LNG (NASDAQ:GLNG) +1.4% in early trading Thursday after saying Southern Energy S.A. reached a final investment decision for the charter of Golar's 3.5M tons/year MK II floating liquefied natural gas vessel, firming up the agreement signed by the two companies in May.

Key commercial terms for the 20-year charter agreement include net charter hire to Golar (NASDAQ:GLNG) of $400M/year, plus a commodity linked tariff component of 25% of free on board prices in excess of $8/MMBtu.

The FLNG, currently under conversion in China, will sail to Argentina following its redelivery, with contract start-up expected in 2028.

The MK II FLNG will be moored in the San Matías Gulf near the FLNG Hilli, which is expected to start its 20-year charter with Southern Energy in 2027; combined, the two units have a nameplate capacity of 5.95M tons/year.

Southern Energy S.A., a company formed to enable LNG exports from Argentina, is owned by a consortium of Argentinian gas producers - Pan American Energy (OTCQB:PAANF), YPF (YPF), Pampa Energia (PAM) and Harbour Energy (OTCPK:PMOIF), plus Golar (GLNG), which holds 10%.

The FID of the MK II FLNG "solidifies $8B of net earnings visibility over 20 years to Golar, with attractive upside potential in the FLNG commodity tariff component and through our shareholding in SESA," Golar (GLNG) CEO Karl Fredrik Staubo said.

data.title: FDA lifts pause on Valneva's chikungunya vaccine in elderly
data.description:
Valneva SE (NASDAQ:VALN) said on Thursday that the FDA has lifted its pause on the use of its chikungunya vaccine, Ixchiq, for individuals aged 60 and older and has updated the prescribing information for the vaccine.

This decision follows a recommendation from the European Medicines Agency in July, which suggested removing temporary restrictions for older adults after a thorough safety review of Ixchiq.

The updated prescribing information now includes details about serious adverse events reported mainly among elderly individuals with multiple health issues, which occurred after a mass vaccination campaign in La Réunion due to a chikungunya outbreak.

The FDA has indicated that the risk of chikungunya virus exposure is low for most U.S. travelers, making the vaccine unadvisable for them. Ixchiq should only be administered when there is a significant risk of infection, and after carefully weighing the benefits and risks, the agency  noted.

Ixchiq is indicated in the U.S. against chikungunya virus in individuals 18 years of age and older who are at high risk of exposure.

data.title: Canaan reports July holdings of 1,511 bitcoins
data.description:
• Canaan (NASDAQ:CAN) said on Thursday that mined 89 bitcoins in the month of July, bringing the total holdings at the month-end to 1,511 bitcoins.
  • It's month-end operating hashrate was 6.24 EH/s while month-end deployed hashrate was 7.95 EH/s.
  • CAN +2.53% premarket to $0.6805.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq leads charge as Trump's chip tariffs come with broad carve-outs
data.description:
Wall Street gained on Thursday, led by the Nasdaq Composite, as traders weighed President Donald Trump's latest chip tariffs, softened by sweeping exemptions.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.5%, the Dow (DJI) +0.5%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.8%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.24%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.73%.

Trump said Wednesday he plans to impose a 100% tariff on semiconductor imports, with an exemption for companies that manufacture or commit to manufacturing in the U.S.

Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) clinched a record high on Thursday after Taiwan said its top chip manufacturer will not have to pay a 100% tariff on such imports to the U.S.

"U.S. manufacturers live in confusing times. Alongside the widespread increases in taxes on imports that took effect this morning, Trump suggested a 100% tax on imports of microchips. However, in a maneuver that was less a retreat and more a pirouette, these taxes will not apply to imports from some specific companies, including some of the largest exporters to the U.S.," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims jumped more than expected and the continuing claims rose to the highest mark since 2021

Labor cost growth cooled markedly in Q2, while productivity rose more than expected.

In more tariff news, Trump hiked the tariff on Indian goods to 50% from 25%, citing India's continued importation of Russian oil.

U.S. stocks rebounded and closed higher on Wednesday, helped by a surge in Apple (AAPL). Traders waded through a slew of quarterly reports from several big companies.

"Tech stocks have continued to drive a buoyant mood in markets, with the Mag-7 reaching a new all-time high. Momentum was also supported by strong earnings and rising hopes of imminent rate cuts as Federal Reserve speakers turned more dovish in response to last Friday’s weak payroll print," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

The upbeat tone has continued overnight despite Trump outlining a plan for 100% tariffs on semiconductors, with the impact of these mitigated by carveouts, Sidorov added.

The June Wholesale Inventories report will land during market hours. No revision is expected in the revised figure from an increase of 0.2 percent in the flash.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Kelly announces selection of Chris Layden as CEO
data.description:
• Kelly (NASDAQ:KELYA) on Thursday appointed Chris Layden as president and chief executive officer, effective September 2.
  • Layden will succeed Peter Quigley, who announced his intent to retire as president and CEO.
  • Quigley will remain as a strategic advisor to Kelly to ensure a smooth transition and will continue to serve as a member of the board of directors until the company’s next Annual Shareholders Meeting in May 2026.
  • Most recently, Layden served as COO of Prolink. Before joining Prolink, Layden spent nearly two decades at ManpowerGroup, serving in a range of senior roles spanning general management, regional leadership, corporate strategy, and sales.
  • KELYA +2.04% premarket to $12.5.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Belite Bio announces registered direct offering of $15 million
data.description:
• Belite Bio (NASDAQ:BLTE) on Thursday said it has entered into a securities purchase agreement for the purchase and sale of 230,770 American Depositary Shares and warrants to purchase 230,770 ADSs, at a purchase price of $65 per ADS and accompanying warrant, in a registered direct offering.
  • The offering is expected to result in gross proceeds of approximately $15 million.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about August 8.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • BLTE +1% premarket to $68.67.
  • Source: Press Release
